series.title: [PATCH] migration: fix QIOChannelFile leak on error in

review.summary: The leak fix in migration/file.c looks correct for both identified error paths, but the added regression test only covers the ftruncate() failure case and does not exercise the qio_channel_io_seek() failure path described in the changelog. The series is close, but test coverage is incomplete for the stated bug scope.

stages.A: Checked the stated regression against the modified error paths in file_connect_outgoing().
stages.B: Validated the added object_unref(OBJECT(fioc)) calls as the correct resource release for the two leak sites.
stages.C: Reviewed the new qtest and confirmed it drives a failing file migration via /dev/full and compares FD counts before and after retries.
stages.D: Cross-checked reported metadata: checkpatch clean, git am applied, configure passed.
stages.E: Overall risk of the code change is low, but the regression coverage does not fully match the commit claim.

findings.title: Regression test covers only one of the two claimed leak paths
findings.description: The changelog says the patch fixes leaks on both the ftruncate() failure path and the later qio_channel_io_seek() failure path, but the new test only drives repeated failures against /dev/full, which exercises the truncate failure case. That leaves the seek-after-channel-creation path untested, so a future regression there would not be caught by this test.
findings.suggestion: Add a second regression test that forces qio_channel_io_seek() to fail after channel creation, or narrow the commit message so it only claims coverage for the truncate-failure leak if that is the only practical test case.
findings.confidence_reason: The exercised path is evident from the use of /dev/full and the commit message explicitly names two separate error sites, but full runtime confirmation of the second path is not available from the diff alone.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
